news.title: Unitech's net loss widens to ₹441 crore in Q4 FY26
news.para: Unitech Ltd reported a consolidated net loss of Rs 441 crore for the quarter ended March 2026, an increase from the previous year. Total income saw a rise in the fourth quarter and for the full fiscal year. The company continues to face a significant cash crunch, leading to project delays and impacting thousands of customers.

news.title: Creditors realised ₹4 lakh crore; cases involving ₹14 lakh crore settled in pre-admission stage: IBC
news.para: India's insolvency law has facilitated over Rs 4 lakh crore in creditor realisations from resolved cases. Additionally, more than 30,000 pre-admission stage cases, involving nearly Rs 14 lakh crore, were settled. This code has significantly altered debtor-creditor dynamics, promoting timely resolution of financial stress.

news.title: No hand raised to buy Ludhiana’s ₹197-crore ghost complex
news.para: A 77-unit derelict property in Ludhiana failed to attract any bidders at auction, marking an 18-year streak of failure for the Ludhiana Improvement Trust. The state-backed developer is now reconsidering the reserve price for the 2.22-acre property after regional officials ignored internal warnings about its inflated valuation, which was expected to fetch ₹197 crore.

news.title: Asia Pacific real estate investment rises 19% in Q1 2026: Report
news.para: Real estate investment in Asia Pacific rose 19.2% year-on-year in Q1 2026, led by office assets and renewed cross-border capital flows, according to Savills. India remained among the markets drawing investor interest in industrial, logistics and data centre-linked assets.

news.title: Delhi draft B&B policy proposes eight room, 16 bed limit; CCTV mandatory
news.para: Delhi is set to introduce a draft policy for Bed and Breakfast units, categorizing them into Silver and Gold. The proposal mandates CCTV cameras, police verification, and adherence to safety and hygiene standards for residential properties offering accommodation. This aims to enhance tourism infrastructure and provide income opportunities for residents.

news.title: Visible road dust, construction waste found during inspection drive in Ghaziabad
news.para: An inspection by the Commission for Air Quality Management (CAQM) in Ghaziabad revealed 24 instances of high visible road dust and construction waste. The commission has directed authorities to intensify dust mitigation measures, including mechanized sweeping and water sprinkling, to address the issue.

news.title: Hyderabad sinks deeper into groundwater crisis, worst-hit metro: Report
news.para: Hyderabad has been identified as India's most critically affected metro for groundwater depletion, with 26 units classified as 'critical' or 'over-exploited'. This alarming situation is attributed to rampant borewell drilling and inadequate rainwater harvesting, pushing water tables to unprecedented depths.

news.title: Chhattisgarh HC halts sand mining tender over incomplete survey report
news.para: The Chhattisgarh High Court has cancelled a sand mining tender in Janjgir-Champa district. The court ruled that mining cannot proceed without an approved District Survey Report. A draft report uploaded online is not sufficient. The Gram Panchayat of Hathnewra had challenged the tender. The court emphasized the need for public objections before finalizing the report.

news.title: E-khata established order in Bengaluru property administration: GBA special commissioner
news.para: Bengaluru e-khata: This digital initiative has brought structure to property dealings, successfully minimising issues related to unapproved layouts and flawed ownership records. Citizens are finding support through the Greater Bengaluru Authority, which champions this government-led effort toward transparency and lawful practices.

news.title: Flexible workspace companies' earnings jump on strong demand, premium shift
news.para: Flexible workspace firms WeWork India, Smartworks, IndiQube, and Awfis reported robust revenue growth and improved margins in the last fiscal year. Sustained demand from global capability centers and a shift to managed offices fueled this performance.

news.title: Australia introduces bill to overhaul property tax breaks, capital gains rule
news.para: Australia's government has introduced significant tax reforms, including ending the capital gains discount and limiting property investor tax breaks, aiming to boost housing affordability. These changes, despite voter backlash and accusations of broken promises, seek to level the playing field for first-home buyers. The reforms will tax inflation-adjusted capital gains and restrict negative gearing to new builds.

news.title: 'Shoebox' flat reform leaves low-income Hong Kong residents in limbo
news.para: Hong Kong resident Lisa Lau put on a costume drama as she settled on the bed that occupies much of her tiny apartment, trying to take her mind off of a looming eviction. The Hong Kong government has given owners who register under the new system until 2030 to renovate their subdivided flats, but some landlords have already issued eviction notices to their tenants.

news.title: Small residential buildings in Delhi seeking regularisation will no longer need physical inspection
news.para: The Municipal Corporation of Delhi has eased building regularisation for residential properties up to 105 square metres.  Owners now submit an architect-certified undertaking online instead of requiring a physical inspection. This aims to streamline approvals, cut delays, and boost civic revenue, making services more accessible for citizens.

news.title: CoStar nears $800 million deal to buy Zonda in housing data push: Sources
news.para: Commercial real estate data firm CoStar Group is close to buying housing market data provider Zonda for nearly eight hundred million dollars. This acquisition will boost CoStar's residential data offerings, particularly in new home construction. Zonda tracks housing starts and builder activity. The deal is expected to be announced soon. CoStar has been expanding through acquisitions.

news.title: Development Bank of Japan invests in HDFC Capital Advisors' H-DREAM Fund
news.para: Development Bank of Japan Inc. has committed capital to HDFC Capital's H-DREAM Fund, marking its first real estate investment in India. This investment, channeled through GIFT City's IFSCA framework, contributes to a platform exceeding $4.5 billion. The H-DREAM Fund targets affordable and mid-income housing projects, incorporating green building standards.

news.title: Arkade Developers' net profit declines 96.63% to ₹5.29 crore in FY26
news.para: Arkade Developers saw its net profit plummet by 96.63% in the financial year 2025-26. Profit after tax fell to ₹5.29 crore from ₹156.92 crore in the previous year. However, total income rose by 19.23% to ₹828.16 crore. The company's pre-sales and collections also showed growth. Arkade Developers has a substantial project pipeline valued at ₹12,800 crore.

news.title: Smartworks to invest ₹70 crore to add 4.92 lakh sq ft office space in Bengaluru
news.para: Smartworks Coworking Spaces is expanding its presence in Bengaluru by leasing 4.92 lakh sq ft at Sattva Aura. This significant expansion will require an investment of approximately ₹70 crore. The company plans to fund this growth through internal accruals or issue proceeds. This move underscores Smartworks' continued expansion in the Indian coworking market.
